fullNotes: On March 19, 2026, ISRO recovered a PSLV SITVC steering component from the Tamil Nadu coast. This steering system uses Strontium Perchlorate injections to nudge the rocket during its first-stage flight. Meanwhile, ISRO is finalizing Chandrayaan-4, a 5-module mission designed to return lunar samples from the 'Mons Mouton' region of the Moon's South Pole by 2028. Future missions like Chandrayaan-5 will feature a 100-day lifespan and a 350kg rover, significantly upgrading India's lunar capabilities.

questions.q: What specific piece of space debris was recovered by ISRO teams off the coast of Tamil Nadu today, March 19, 2026?
questions.explanation: The SITVC stands for Secondary Injection Thrust Vector Control, a steering component from a PSLV booster.

questions.q: Which part of the rocket does this SITVC system belong to?
questions.explanation: It is part of the S139 solid-fuel motor that provides initial thrust at liftoff.

questions.q: What chemical is 'squirted' through ports in the SITVC system to help the rocket steer during flight?
questions.explanation: Injecting this chemical disrupts exhaust flow to nudge the rocket left, right, up, or down.

questions.q: ISRO is recovering this debris as part of its 'Debris Free Space Mission.' What is the target year for India to achieve zero new space debris?
questions.explanation: India aims for zero new debris by 2030 through better de-orbiting and recovery efforts.

questions.q: Moving to future missions, what is the primary objective of the upcoming Chandrayaan-4 mission?
options: Landing a human on the Moon
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-4 aims to collect lunar soil and bring it safely back to Earth for the first time.

questions.q: How many distinct modules will make up the complex Chandrayaan-4 spacecraft to achieve this return trip?
questions.explanation: It includes Propulsion, Descender, Ascender, Transfer, and Re-entry modules.

questions.q: Which specific region on the Moon's South Pole has been finalized as the landing site for Chandrayaan-4?
questions.explanation: Mons Mouton was chosen for its gentle slopes and safety for precision touchdown.

questions.q: While Chandrayaan-3's lander lived for 14 days, what is the intended mission life for the future Chandrayaan-5?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-5 is being designed for a much longer operational life on the lunar surface.

questions.q: How much will the upgraded rover of the future Chandrayaan missions weigh compared to the 25kg Pragyan rover?
questions.explanation: The new rover will be substantially larger to carry more scientific instruments.

questions.q: Which heavy-lift rocket is being upgraded with a 22-tonne thrust engine to support these ambitious missions?
questions.explanation: The LVM3 (formerly GSLV Mk III) is India's 'Bahubali' rocket used for heavy missions.

questions.q: What is India's target year for landing its first 'Gaganyatri' (astronaut) on the lunar surface?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Modi has set a target for an Indian moon landing by 2040.
